_about: The scorecard headline numbers most prone to the 'how much we do of X but X != market' trap. industry coverage is SELF-REFERENTIAL (our taxonomy) and must be fenced; concept coverage fixes the denominator to the whole discovered space (external-universe) and so is honest by shape - the teaching contrast the PM review surfaced.

rows.invited_intent: fak covers / serves ~100% of the LLM-serving market
rows.literal: 100% = 89/89 industry dimensions fak has POSITIONED itself against in its OWN taxonomy; the standing is 5 lead / 11 parity / 8 trails / 66 honest gaps - it grades map completeness, not market share or wins

rows.invited_intent: fak is an A-grade product - fak is winning the field
rows.literal: 98.8/100 grades how complete and honest fak's competitive MAP is (honesty x 60% + coverage x 40%), explicitly not how much fak wins

rows.invited_intent: the whole discovered confusable namespace is now positioned
rows.literal: 100.0% = 862/862 confusable tree tokens positioned; the denominator is the whole discovered confusable space (not a hand-picked set), so the high number is the honest 'the known confusable namespace is now positioned' - the positioned ones carry zero clarity defects
gaps: Contrast row: this denominator is the discovered universe, the honest fix to the self-referential framing industry-coverage exposes.
